designNotes.theory: ACTFL 90% TL + Krashen i+1 + CLIL translanguaging
designNotes.chineseRole: emotional connection, key concept bridging, cultural context — kept simple for children
designNotes.englishLevel: simple sentences, familiar vocabulary, short paragraphs, present tense dominant
designNotes.boldWords: target vocabulary for FSRS review, clickable in UI

agentReasoning.environmentInfluence: Sunny day, light and welcoming tone
agentReasoning.topicChoice: Welcome letter — build trust, introduce the letter mechanic

body: Hello! I am Capybara. I am a big, friendly animal who loves to **travel**.
Every night, I go on an **adventure**. I visit new places and learn new things.
Then I write you a letter about what I saw!
你可以点击信里的粗体词，看看它们是什么意思。
Tomorrow, tell me what you want to know. Dinosaurs? The moon? The ocean? Anything!
I will go there and write you a letter about it.
Ready? See you tomorrow!
letter.postscript: P.S. Tell me below — where should I go tonight?

vocabularyCards.example: I am a big, friendly animal who loves to travel.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 我是一只喜欢旅行的大动物。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Capybara's favorite thing to do!

vocabularyCards.example: Every night, I go on an adventure.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 每天晚上我都去冒险。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Where will Capybara go tonight?

environment.parentNote: 今天学校春游去了动物园，看了长颈鹿和大象

eveningWish.capybaraReply: Dinosaurs! OK, tonight I will travel to a very old time. I heard there are animals bigger than houses there. See you tomorrow!

chat.speaker: capybara
chat.text: Some are taller than your building! I will tell you in my letter. Good night!

agentReasoning.wishInfluence: Child wants to know about dinosaurs
agentReasoning.environmentInfluence: Parent note: school trip to zoo today — bridge from zoo animals to ancient relatives
agentReasoning.topicChoice: Zoo animals have ancient relatives — connect today's experience to dinosaurs naturally

body: I heard you went to the zoo today! Did you see the **giraffe** and the **elephant**?
你知道吗？Those big animals have very, very old relatives.
Last night, I traveled to a place called the Mesozoic Era. 那是很久很久以前的时代。
There were no fences. No tickets. Just huge **dinosaurs** walking everywhere!
One dinosaur was called Brachiosaurus. Its neck was three times longer than a giraffe! It was as **tall** as a four-story building.
And guess what? Some dinosaurs were tiny — only as big as a **chicken**! They had feathers on their body.
Scientists think birds came from dinosaurs. 所以你今天在动物园看到的鸟，可能就是恐龙的后代！
Tomorrow I want to explore more. What do you want to know?
letter.postscript: P.S. Next time at the zoo, look at a bird's feet — do they look like tiny dinosaur claws?

vocabularyCards.example: Did you see the giraffe at the zoo?
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 你在动物园看到长颈鹿了吗？
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Its neck is very long — but not as long as Brachiosaurus!

vocabularyCards.example: Huge dinosaurs were walking everywhere!
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 巨大的恐龙到处走！
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Which dinosaur do you want to meet?

vocabularyCards.example: It was as tall as a four-story building.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 它和四层楼一样高。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Look up — can you imagine something that tall?

vocabularyCards.example: Some dinosaurs were only as big as a chicken!
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 有些恐龙只有鸡那么大！
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Small dinosaurs with feathers became birds!

research.title: Birds evolved from dinosaurs
research.summary: Modern birds are direct descendants of theropod dinosaurs. Archaeopteryx is one of the earliest known transitional fossils, from about 150 million years ago.

eveningWish.capybaraReply: Big question! Tonight I will travel to 65 million years ago. I heard something fell from the sky that day. Let me go see!

agentReasoning.wishInfluence: Child asks why dinosaurs went extinct
agentReasoning.environmentInfluence: Rainy day — use rain atmosphere to echo the post-impact darkness
agentReasoning.topicChoice: The asteroid that changed everything — storytelling approach, not encyclopedia

body: Is it raining outside today? The place I visited last night was raining too — but that rain lasted for years.
65 million years ago, a giant **asteroid** fell from the sky. It was as big as a mountain!
It hit the Earth so hard that **dust** covered the whole sky. The sun could not shine through.
没有阳光，plants could not grow. Without plants, dinosaurs had nothing to eat.
But not everything died! Some tiny **mammals** — smaller than a mouse — hid underground and survived.
还记得昨天说的吗？Those small feathered dinosaurs? Some of them **survived** too. They became birds!
So even though the big dinosaurs are gone, their story continues. Every bird you see is a living dinosaur story.
下次下雨的时候，想想那些躲在地洞里等待的小动物——它们后来变成了我们！
letter.postscript: P.S. The mammals that survived were so small they could fit in your hand. 后来它们变成了我们人类！

vocabularyCards.example: A giant asteroid fell from the sky.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 一颗巨大的小行星从天上掉下来。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: A space rock bigger than a mountain!

vocabularyCards.example: Dust covered the whole sky.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 灰尘遮住了整个天空。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: So much dust that the sun disappeared!

vocabularyCards.example: Tiny mammals hid underground and survived.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 小小的哺乳动物躲在地下活了下来。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: We are mammals too!

vocabularyCards.example: Some feathered dinosaurs survived.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 一些有羽毛的恐龙活了下来。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: They were tough little creatures!

research.title: Cretaceous-Paleogene extinction event
research.summary: About 66 million years ago, a 10km asteroid hit what is now Mexico. The global disaster killed about 75% of all species, including all non-bird dinosaurs.

eveningWish.capybaraReply: Hmm, we talked about dinosaurs for two days already. Today is Qingming — a day for remembering. Let me show you how nature remembers things too — fossils! They are like letters from millions of years ago.

chat.speaker: capybara
chat.text: Imagine you step in mud. Now imagine that footprint turns to stone and stays there for 100 million years. That is a fossil!

agentReasoning.wishInfluence: Child wants more dinosaurs, but 2 days in a row — need new angle to avoid echo chamber
agentReasoning.environmentInfluence: Qingming Festival (remembering the past) → natural bridge to fossils (nature's way of remembering)
agentReasoning.topicChoice: Fossils: nature's letters — connects dinosaur interest + Qingming theme + introduces geology

body: Today is Qingming Festival. 人们会去看望不在了的亲人，remember them.
Nature has its own way of remembering — **fossils**.
A fossil is like a letter from nature. A dinosaur stepped in mud millions of years ago, and that footprint turned to **stone**.
Millions of years later, we open this 'letter' and learn how big the dinosaur was, how fast it walked.
Last night I went to a **museum** basement. (Don't tell anyone!) There was a complete Triceratops **skull** — bigger than your desk!
Scientists can read fossils like letters. From the shape of bones, they can guess what dinosaurs ate, how fast they ran, even what diseases they had.
虽然恐龙不在了，but they left many 'letters' for us to read. Just like Qingming helps us remember family, fossils help us remember Earth's story.
letter.signoff: Sneaking out of the museum basement, Capybara
letter.postscript: P.S. Have you ever found a strange rock outside? Maybe there is an ancient letter inside it!

vocabularyCards.example: A fossil is like a letter from nature.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 化石就像大自然写的信。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: A delivery from millions of years ago!

vocabularyCards.example: That footprint turned to stone.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 那个脚印变成了石头。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Hard as a rock — because it IS a rock!

vocabularyCards.example: I went to a museum basement last night.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 我昨晚去了博物馆地下室。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Ask your parents to take you to see real fossils!

vocabularyCards.example: The Triceratops skull was bigger than your desk!
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 三角龙的头骨比你的桌子还大！
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Triceratops had three horns on its head

research.title: Fossils
research.summary: Fossils are preserved remains or traces of ancient organisms in rock. The most common formation is mineralization: minerals gradually replace organic material, creating a stone copy.

eveningWish.capybaraReply: Space! I need to fly very far tonight. Remember the asteroid that hit the dinosaurs? It came from space. Let me go find where it came from!

agentReasoning.wishInfluence: Child wants to know about space
agentReasoning.environmentInfluence: No special event — bridge from dinosaur asteroid to space naturally
agentReasoning.topicChoice: Where did the asteroid come from? — connect previous dinosaur story to space exploration

body: Remember the **asteroid** that hit the dinosaurs? It came from space. So last night, I flew up to find its home.
Space is very, very **empty**. Most of it is just darkness and silence. 安静得一点声音都没有。
But sometimes, you see a **planet** glowing far away. I flew past Mars — it is red because the soil has iron in it. 就像铁生锈了一样！
Then I reached the asteroid belt. Thousands of big rocks **floating** in space, slowly turning.
The asteroid that hit Earth came from somewhere like this.
And here is the most amazing thing: scientists found **water** on some asteroids! Water — on rocks in space!
Some people think Earth's water came from asteroids long ago. 所以灭绝恐龙的小行星是坏的，但更早的小行星可能给地球带来了水。
Interesting, right? Tonight, look up at the sky. Between those stars, there are many rocks flying.
letter.signoff: From the asteroid belt (signal is weak here), Capybara
letter.postscript: P.S. The water you drink might have come from space! 你喝的水可能来自太空！

vocabularyCards.example: Space is very, very empty.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 太空非常非常空。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: So empty there is no sound at all

vocabularyCards.example: You see a planet glowing far away.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 你看到一颗行星在远处发光。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Earth is a planet too!

vocabularyCards.example: Big rocks floating in space, slowly turning.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 大石头在太空中漂浮，慢慢转。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Everything floats in space — no gravity!

vocabularyCards.example: Scientists found water on some asteroids.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 科学家在小行星上发现了水。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Your water might be from space!

research.title: Asteroid belt
research.summary: The asteroid belt sits between Mars and Jupiter, containing millions of asteroids. Missions in the 2010s found water ice and hydrated minerals on several asteroids.

environment.parentNote: 周末带去了科技馆，看了火箭模型

eveningWish.capybaraReply: Rockets! I saw many rockets flying past me in space last night. Let me go find a rocket factory tonight!

chat.speaker: capybara
chat.text: Really! Did you see the rocket model? My letter is coming soon — it has secrets you did not see at the museum!

agentReasoning.wishInfluence: Child wants to know how rockets fly
agentReasoning.environmentInfluence: Parent note: visited science museum, saw rocket models — connect to real experience
agentReasoning.topicChoice: How rockets fly — Newton's Third Law as a story, with balloon experiment

body: I heard you went to the science museum today! Did you see the big rocket model?
Have you ever wondered: rockets are so **heavy** — how can they fly up?
The secret is one word: **push**.
Try this: stand on a skateboard and push a wall. You push the wall, the wall pushes you back — and you slide away!
Rockets work the same way! The rocket **burns** fuel at the bottom. Hot gas shoots down. 气体往下推，rocket pushes up!
This is called Newton's Third Law: every push has an equal push back. 每一个力都有一个反作用力。
The rocket model at the museum was quiet and still. But a real rocket launch? The ground **shakes** and the sound is so loud you feel it in your chest!
Try this tonight: blow up a balloon, don't tie it, let it go. Watch it fly! 那就是一个小小的气球火箭。
letter.signoff: From the launch pad (my ears are still ringing), Capybara
letter.postscript: P.S. Blow up a balloon and let it go — you just made a balloon rocket!

vocabularyCards.example: Rockets are so heavy — how can they fly?
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 火箭那么重——怎么能飞？
vocabularyCards.tapHint: A rocket weighs more than 100 elephants!

vocabularyCards.example: You push the wall, the wall pushes you back.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 你推墙，墙也推你。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Push and push back — that's the rocket secret

vocabularyCards.example: The rocket burns fuel at the bottom.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 火箭在底部燃烧燃料。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Rocket fuel burns hotter than any fire

vocabularyCards.example: The ground shakes when a rocket launches.
vocabularyCards.exampleZh: 火箭发射时地面会震动。
vocabularyCards.tapHint: Like a tiny earthquake!

research.title: Newton's Third Law of Motion
research.summary: Newton's Third Law states: when one object pushes another, the second object pushes back with equal force in the opposite direction. Rocket propulsion uses this principle.

chat.speaker: child
chat.text: 卡皮巴拉你今天去哪了

chat.speaker: capybara
chat.text: Today I stayed home! I am looking at all the things we learned this week. You learned so many new words! Want to review them together?

chat.speaker: capybara
chat.text: Great! Let me get all the word cards out. My letter tonight is a special review story too.

agentReasoning.wishInfluence: No new wish, only casual chat
agentReasoning.environmentInfluence: Sunday — good day for review and rest
agentReasoning.topicChoice: Weekly review letter — connect all topics (zoo → dinosaurs → fossils → space → rockets), reinforce vocabulary

body: This is our first week together! Let me remember where we went:
Tuesday: we started at the zoo and met the dinosaurs — those ancient **relatives** of today's animals.
Wednesday: we saw the giant **asteroid** that changed everything. Tiny **mammals** survived underground.
Thursday: we read nature's letters — **fossils** in the **museum**.
Friday: we flew to **empty** space and found **water** on floating rocks.
Saturday: we learned the secret of rockets — **push** and **shakes**!
One whole week of adventures! Which day was your favorite?
Next week we can go anywhere. The ocean? The jungle? Back to space? Tell me what you want to know!
letter.signoff: Packing my bag at home, Capybara
letter.postscript: P.S. You learned so many words this week! Review the cards below — how many do you remember?
